## Overview

md2terminal is a terminal-based markdown viewer. It is primariliy used to view reports generated by tfplan2md (https://github.com/oocx/tfplan2md). 

## Comments

Use comments to explain why something is done, not what is done. The code itself should be clear enough to explain what is being done. Comments should be used to provide context, rationale, or any non-obvious information that cannot be easily inferred from the code.

All class members must have comments.


## Design Principles

Use the following principles:
- SOLID
- YAGNI
- KISS
- One Level of Abstraction


## Code Style

- Use .NET Coding Conventions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/fundamentals/coding-style/coding-conventions
- Use "using static" for static classes to improve readability
- Import common namespaces globally in the project
- Keep files short and focused. If a file exceeds 300 lines, refactoring it into smaller files.

## Mandatory Documentation

- Track all architecture decisions in docs/architecture.md
- Track all requirements in docs/specification.md
- Log your work in docs/worklog.md
- README.md must provide a general project overview and usage information.
- Use the documents in /docs to remember important details and decisions that must still be available for future sessions.

## Additional references

- Read docs/dotnet-csharp.md for a refresher on .NET 10 and C# features. Mandatory to read if your training data does not include .NET 10 / C# 14.
- A list of example reports can be found at https://github.com/oocx/tfplan2md/tree/main/artifacts. md2terminal must support all markdown and html constructs used in these reports.

## Non-Functional Requirements

- Security: Ensure that the code is secure and does not expose vulnerabilities. This is your top priority.
- Maintainability: Write clean, modular, and well-documented code that is easy to understand and maintain. The code must be easy to understand for LLMs and humans alike.
- User Experience: The tool must have a great user experience and a beautiful, aestetic user interface.
